channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Residential bins on street always overflowing, 20th June
item.description:
Residential bins are permanently kept on street and always overflowing. The pavement is filthy and strewn with garbage. There is a Christmas tree in one bin that hasn’t been emptied in months.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lauriston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street sweeping request, 20th June
item.description:
Street is messy , lots of rubbish in gutter
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Shenley Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo on pavement outside railings, 20th June
item.description:
Outside 2 Shenley Road (grey railings)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Shenley Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 20th June
item.description:
Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, New Kent Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sweeping vehicle malfunction, 20th June
item.description:
There appears to be a problem with your road sweeping vehicle. Debris from gutters is being repositioned into the road. This is likely to be an issue for cyclists with a risk of puncture increased. Along the entirety of Aldersmead Road, BR3 1NA.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aldersmead Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 20th June
item.description:
Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New Kent Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree keeps falling over, 20th June
item.description:
This tree has been corrected before but is not stable. It seems the ground is too disturbed to support the tree.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Goldsmith Road, Friern Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wooden pallet dumped, Corner of Deverell St + Spurgeon St, 20th June
item.description:
Wooden pallet dumped - see photo

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Deverell Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public parking putting bins to save space, 20th June
item.description:
People cannot park on free carpark as they always put their bins to save space. This has been reprted several times. This is a public car space

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wincrofts Drive, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Half dismantled bike, 20th June
item.description:
Locked and abandoned for ages to post

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Half Moon Lane, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 9 mattress, 20th June
item.description:
9 mattress
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dudbrook Road Lady's Hill, Navestock, Brentwood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Seven Dials road junction itself, 20th June
item.description:
The monument is supposed to be the centre of a roundabout but only 1 of the three roads entering it has the necessary sign. Vehicles entering from Mercer St North or Monmouth Street South sometimes go the wrong way round it. This could cause an accident with road users, including cyclists and pedestrians, who are expecting it's use as a roundabout
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seven Dials, Bloomsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pathway/road repairs required, 20th June
item.description:
Please can these large dips in the road be filled in. When it rains people who are less abled or who have prams are unable to get into the park due to the dips filling up with very muddy water. Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog poo, unresolved, 20th June
item.description:
Big poo by the cycle hangar
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shacklewell Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing large puddles/mud, 20th June
item.description:
When it rains this area of the park cannot be used by less abled people, people in wheelchairs, less abled people or those pushing a pram. Please can the roadway be fixed and the dips filled in so that the park can be accessible all year around. Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Teenagers throwing branches/rocks into moat, 20th June
item.description:
Teenagers have been throwing branches and rocks at the nesting birds in the moat. Please can we have extra patrols.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Teenagers causing damage to ice house & bird boxes, 20th June
item.description:
Most afternoons around 3pm - 6pm we have groups of teenagers ‘smoking’, drinking, causing criminal damage to The Ice House, writing graffiti on The Ice House, damaging the fence near The Ive House and now they have started knocking the bird boxes off trees near The Ice House. Please can we have extra patrols? The only time we haven’t had damage is when the bench from inside The Ice House was removed for 24 hours. Maybe permanent removal of the bench is something to consider moving forward.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass needs cleaning away, 20th June
item.description:
Please can this broken vodka bottle be cleaned away before someone or something gets hurt. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damage to ceiling of The Ice House, 20th June
item.description:
Please can this hole in the ceiling of The Ice House be repaired. Many thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on ceiling of The Ice House, 20th June
item.description:
Please can this graffiti be removed or painted over. It’s on the ceiling of The Ice House. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
